data.title: EchoStar Stock Down on Wider Loss, Lower Sales in 2Q
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Shares of EchoStar declined after the company posted a wider loss and lower revenue in the second quarter.

The stock fell 17% to $27.16 on Friday. Shares are up 19% this year.

The owner of Dish TV and Boost Mobile posted a loss of $306.1 million, or $1.06 a share, compared with a loss of $205.6 million, or 76 cents a share, a year earlier. Analysts expected a loss of 93 cents a share.

Revenue fell 5.8% to $3.72 billion, missing the $3.83 billion expected by analysts.

The company's Pay-TV segment led the sales decline, down 8% to $2.46 billion. Broadband and satellite services sales also fell, while wireless revenue rose.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Colgate-Palmolive to Lean More Heavily on AI to Boost Productivity — Market Talk
data.description: Colgate-Palmolive is embarking on restructuring that it says will in part streamline its organizational structure and optimize its global supply chain. Under the so-called productivity program, the maker of Ajax dish soap and Speed Stick deodorants will also invest more heavily in emerging technologies like artificial intelligence. "AI will be a difference maker for us in our revenue-growth management efforts, as we work with our retail partners to use data analytics and machine learning to optimize our portfolio and promotional spending to solve for the best combination of sales and profit growth," CEO Noel Wallace says on a call with analysts. Colgate expects to record $200 million to $300 million in charges over the next three years as a result of the program. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

data.title: EchoStar Down Over 18%, on Pace for Largest Percent Decrease Since November 2023 — Data Talk
data.description:
EchoStar Corporation Class A (SATS) is currently at $26.60, down $5.99 or 18.38%

  • Would be lowest close since June 26, 2025, when it closed at $25.46
  • On pace for largest percent decrease since Nov. 6, 2023, when it fell 31.28%
  • Down 10.35% this week; worst weekly performance since the week ending May 23, 2025, when it fell 15.52%
  • Currently down three of the past four days
  • Currently down two consecutive days; down 18.95% over this period
  • Worst two-day stretch since the two days ending Aug. 12, 2024, when it fell 22.81%
  • Up 16.16% year-to-date
  • Down 47.25% from its all-time closing high of $50.43 on June 19, 2017
  • Up 36.41% from 52 weeks ago (Aug. 2, 2024), when it closed at $19.50
  • Down 18.95% from its 52-week closing high of $32.82 on July 30, 2025
  • Up 71.61% from its 52-week closing low of $15.50 on Aug. 12, 2024
  • Traded as low as $26.14; lowest intraday level since June 27, 2025, when it hit $25.64
  • Down 19.78% at today's intraday low; largest intraday percent decrease since May 12, 2025, when it fell as much as 24.06%

All data as of 11:48:55 AM ET

Source: Dow Jones Market Data, FactSet

data.title: CNH Industrial posts upbeat results, but tariffs cloud the rest of 2025
data.description:
Farm and construction equipment maker CNH Industrial expects a muted finish to the year due to tariff impacts, despite posting second-quarter results above expectations on Friday.

The Basildon, UK-based company said most units sold in the second quarter had limited impact from additional tariffs.

The effects will come through more in the second half of the year as tariff-affected inventory flows through the production system, CFO James Nikolas said.

CEO Gerrit Marx said in a post earnings call that much will depend on the scope and timing of U.S. tariffs and how trade partners respond.

CNH plans to offset future tariff costs through more price hikes and cheaper sourcing.

It expects 2025 sales to drop below last year's levels, but reaffirmed its annual forecasts.

While the company is now tracking ahead of its full-year guidance, tariff impact is weighted to the second half of the year, Oppenheimer analyst Kristen Owen said.

The company had reported second-quarter profit and revenue above Wall Street estimates earlier on Friday, helped by stronger pricing and cost cutting.

It reported an adjusted profit of 17 cents per share for the quarter ended June 30, above analysts' expectations of 14 cents per share, according to data complied by LSEG.

Its total costs and expenses in the reported quarter dropped to $4.43 billion, from $5.03 billion last year.

Quarterly revenue fell 14% to $4.71 billion, but was above analysts' estimates of $4.17 billion.

CNH has been producing tractors and combines below retail demand to reduce excess dealer inventories in a cyclical period of lower sales – a strategy also adopted by peers Deere , AGCO and Caterpillar .

It said it will align its production levels with retail demand by 2026.

data.title: Colliers Intl Group Is Maintained at Outperform by RBC Capital
Advent International Raises Bid for Spectris to $5.55 Billion — Update
data.description:
By Andrea Figueras

Advent International said it increased its takeover bid for precision-measurement company Spectris to 4.2 billion pounds ($5.55 billion).

The deal comes roughly a month after Spectris agreed to a 4.1 billion pound takeover by private-equity firm Kohlberg Kravis Roberts & Co. At the time, Spectris withdrew its support for a previously agreed upon takeover offer from Advent International for 3.8 billion pounds.

Spectris said Friday that its directors decided unanimously to withdraw their recommendation of the KKR offer and support the new offer from Advent.

Under the increased offer by Advent, accepting shareholders would get 40.72 pounds in cash for each Spectris share and an interim dividend of 28 pence for fiscal 2025.

This is an improvement on the KKR deal, which was for 39.72 pounds in cash for each Spectris share and an interim dividend of 28 pence.

KKR said it is considering its position and will make a further announcement when appropriate. In the meantime, it advised Spectris shareholders to take no action in response to the revised Advent offer.

Write to Andrea Figueras at andrea.figueras@wsj.com

data.title: Fed spearheads effort to ease 'Basel III endgame' capital requirements, Bloomberg News reports
data.description:
The Federal Reserve has begun developing a new risk-based capital rule aimed at easing the burden on the largest U.S. banks than a Biden-era plan, Bloomberg News reported on Friday, citing people familiar with the matter.

Regulators are largely scrapping the original 1,087-page proposal from two years ago and plan to unveil a new one as early as the first quarter of 2026, the report added.

Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman is leading efforts to develop the new rule, that would aim to simplify how banks calculate their capital requirements, according to the report.

Representatives of the Federal Reserve declined to comment, when contacted by Reuters.

The "Basel III" standard was agreed after the 2007-09 global financial crisis. It includes numerous capital, leverage and liquidity requirements. Regulators across the world have worked for years to implement many of those standards, and the so-called "endgame," agreed in 2017, is the final iteration.

These capital requirements serve as a financial cushion to enhance the resilience of these big banks against economic stress and safeguard the broader financial system.

The banking industry lobbied for a significantly weaker version of the "Basel Endgame" capital rule under the Trump administration, which was viewed as more favorable compared to the Biden-era approach.

The proposed rules, which would apply to banks with more than $100 billion in assets, would overhaul how the largest banks manage their capital, with potential ripple effects on their lending and trading activities.

The rules aim to prevent another financial crisis by requiring the largest U.S. banks, including JPMorgan Chase , Bank of America , and Goldman Sachs , to collectively set aside nearly $1 trillion to absorb potential losses from loans and trading activities.

However, banks say additional capital is unnecessary and will hurt the economy, and have aggressively lobbied against the project.

data.title: Melco Resorts & Entertainment Ltd reports results for the quarter ended June 30 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Melco Resorts & Entertainment Ltd reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 1 cent​​ per share for the quarter ended June 30, lower than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 5 cents. The mean expectation of six analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 9 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 1 cents to 17 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 14.5% to $1.33 billion from a year ago; analysts expected $1.25 billion.

  • Melco Resorts & Entertainment Ltd's reported EPS for the quarter was 1 cent​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $17.19 million.

  • Melco Resorts & Entertainment Ltd shares had risen by 19.6% this quarter and gained 49.4% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 99.2% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no negative revisions of earnings estimates

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 8 "strong buy" or "buy," 5 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the casinos & gaming peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Melco Resorts & Entertainment Ltd is $9.55, about 9.4% above its last closing price of $8.65

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data August 1 at 03:49 p.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: Brazil's EMS to sell injector pens to treat obesity
data.description:
Brazilian pharmaceutical company EMS on Friday said that local pharmacies will start selling its injector pens aimed at treating diabetes and obesity from August 4.

The pens, made in Brazil, uses liraglutide as an active ingredient, like Saxenda, from Novo Nordisk , which has demonstrated consistent benefits in controlling blood glucose and weight, EMS said.

Saxenda is part of the same class of medication as Nordisk's popular Ozempic.

A total of 100,000 Olire pens, for the treatment of obesity, and 50,000 Lirux pens, for the control of type 2 diabetes, will be available at select pharmacy chains in Brazil.

The launch of both medications comes as Brazilian companies, including EMS and rival Hypera Pharma , gear up to launch semaglutide medications next year, as Ozempic's patent in the country is expected to expire in March.

data.title: Disney Currently Down Seven Consecutive Days, on Track for Longest Losing Streak Since July 2024 — Data Talk
data.description:
Walt Disney Company (DIS) is currently at $116.58, down $2.53 or 2.12%

  • Would be lowest close since June 9, 2025, when it closed at $115.66
  • On pace for largest percent decrease since April 16, 2025, when it fell 2.63%
  • Down 4.03% this week; worst weekly performance since the week ending April 4, 2025, when it fell 14.83%
  • Currently down seven consecutive days; down 5.17% over this period
  • Longest losing streak since July 25, 2024, when it fell for seven straight trading days
  • Worst seven day stretch since the seven days ending April 21, 2025, when it fell 8.14%
  • Up 4.7% year-to-date
  • Down 42.26% from its all-time closing high of $201.91 on March 8, 2021
  • Up 30.16% from 52 weeks ago (Aug. 2, 2024), when it closed at $89.57
  • Down 5.99% from its 52-week closing high of $124.01 on June 30, 2025
  • Up 42.66% from its 52-week closing low of $81.72 on April 8, 2025
  • Traded as low as $115.57; lowest intraday level since June 23, 2025, when it hit $115.02
  • Down 2.97% at today's intraday low; largest intraday percent decrease since May 5, 2025, when it fell as much as 3.11%
  • 10th worst performer in the DJIA today
  • Subtracted 15.55 points from the DJIA so far today

All data as of 11:25:09 AM ET

Source: Dow Jones Market Data, FactSet

data.title: US Gasoline Futures Fall Further
data.description:
US gasoline futures fell toward $2.10 per gallon, extending their retreat from the over five-week high of $2.22 reached on July 29, as mounting concerns over US economic growth dampened the outlook for fuel demand.

A slew of disappointing economic data, including slowing job gains and the sharpest contraction in factory activity in nine months, signaled that the US economy may be losing steam.

This, coupled with fresh tariff announcements from President Donald Trump, fueled uncertainty and dragged down energy markets broadly.

Gasoline followed crude lower, as traders worried about weaker consumption and possible oversupply.

Meanwhile, OPEC+ is set to meet this weekend, and expectations of a 548,000 bpd output boost are adding to bearish sentiment.
